text: I need to net one birdie only And I will stop feeling so lonely. But if she won't fly to my aid, Then into a ghost I must fade. I need to net one birdie only But if she won't fly to my aid, Then into a ghost I must fade.  To a ghost I must fade! To a ghost I must fade!
en: I need to net one birdie only And I will stop feeling so lonely. But if she won't fly to my aid, Then into a ghost I must fade. I need to net one birdie only But if she won't fly to my aid, Then into a ghost I must fade.  To a ghost I must fade! To a ghost I must fade!

text: A sweetheart or a pretty little wife is Papageno's wish. A willing, billing, lovey dovey Would be My most tasty little dish.
en: A sweetheart or a pretty little wife is Papageno's wish. A willing, billing, lovey dovey Would be My most tasty little dish.

text: At present the girls only peck me. Their cruelty surely will wreck me. But one little beak in my own, And I'll up to heaven be flown! At present the girls only peck me. But one little beak in my own, And I'll up to heaven be flown. Up to heaven be flown! Up to heaven be flown!
en: At present the girls only peck me. Their cruelty surely will wreck me. But one little beak in my own, And I'll up to heaven be flown! At present the girls only peck me. But one little beak in my own, And I'll up to heaven be flown. Up to heaven be flown! Up to heaven be flown!

text: What? Who the devil are you?
en: What? Who the devil are you?

text: I've taken pity on you, my angel. I heard your wish.
en: I've taken pity on you, my angel. I heard your wish.

text: Oh. Well, thank you! How wonderful. Some people get all the luck.
en: Oh. Well, thank you! How wonderful. Some people get all the luck.

text: I can't wait.
en: I can't wait.

text: Well, promise then.
en: Well, promise then.

text: What do you mean - now?
en: What do you mean - now?

text: Of course now. Right away, before I get any older.
en: Of course now. Right away, before I get any older.

text: Oh, I'm tender enough for you, my boy. I'm tender enough for you.
en: Oh, I'm tender enough for you, my boy. I'm tender enough for you.

text: I'm taking you home. You're not well.
en: I'm taking you home. You're not well.

text: No, no. I have to get back. I have -
en: No, no. I have to get back. I have -

text: Not here! She's not well, either. She went to the Spa.
en: Not here! She's not well, either. She went to the Spa.

text: You mean she's not coming back?
en: You mean she's not coming back?

text: You're so good to me. Truly. Thank you.
en: You're so good to me. Truly. Thank you.

text: No, please.
en: No, please.

text: I mean to come to my opera. You are the only colleague who did.
en: I mean to come to my opera. You are the only colleague who did.

text: This is only a vaudeville.
en: This is only a vaudeville.

text: Oh no. It is a sublime piece. The grandest operone. I tell you, you are the greatest composer known to me.
en: Oh no. It is a sublime piece. The grandest operone. I tell you, you are the greatest composer known to me.

text: Do you mean that?
en: Do you mean that?

text: I do.
en: I do.

text: I have bad fancies. I don't sleep well anymore. Then I drink too much, and think stupid things.
en: I have bad fancies. I don't sleep well anymore. Then I drink too much, and think stupid things.

text: Are you ill?
en: Are you ill?

text: The doctor thinks I am. But -
en: The doctor thinks I am. But -

text: What?
en: What?

text: I'm too young to be so sick.
en: I'm too young to be so sick.

text: No! No, it's him!
en: No! No, it's him!

text: Who?
en: Who?

text: The man. He's here.
en: The man. He's here.

text: What man?
en: What man?

text: Finish what?
en: Finish what?

text: He knows. He knows!
en: He knows. He knows!

text: Yes, I am looking after him.
en: Yes, I am looking after him.

text: Can we come in?
en: Can we come in?

text: Well, he's sleeping now. Better not.
en: Well, he's sleeping now. Better not.

text: But he's all right?
en: But he's all right?

text: Oh, yes. He's just exhausted. He became dizzy, that's all. We should let him rest.
en: Oh, yes. He's just exhausted. He became dizzy, that's all. We should let him rest.

text: Well, tell him we were here, won't you?
en: Well, tell him we were here, won't you?

text: Of course.
en: Of course.

text: And say everything went wonderfully. A triumph-de-luxe - say that! Tell him the audience shouted his name a hundred times.
en: And say everything went wonderfully. A triumph-de-luxe - say that! Tell him the audience shouted his name a hundred times.

text: Bene.
en: Bene.

text: I'll call tomorrow.
en: I'll call tomorrow.

text: Yes.  And congratulations to all of you. It was superb.
en: Yes.  And congratulations to all of you. It was superb.

text: Yes, indeed. Goodnight to you all now. It was perfection - truly!
en: Yes, indeed. Goodnight to you all now. It was perfection - truly!

text: Yes. Can I - could I help you, in any way?
en: Yes. Can I - could I help you, in any way?

text: Would you? Actually, you could.
en: Would you? Actually, you could.

text: My dear friend, it would be my greatest pleasure.
en: My dear friend, it would be my greatest pleasure.

text: But you'd have to swear not to tell a soul. I'm not allowed.
en: But you'd have to swear not to tell a soul. I'm not allowed.

text: Of course.
en: Of course.

text: You know, it's all here in my head. It's just ready to be set down. But when I'm dizzy like this my eyes won't focus. I can't write.
en: You know, it's all here in my head. It's just ready to be set down. But when I'm dizzy like this my eyes won't focus. I can't write.

text: Then, let us try together. I'd regard it as such an honour. Tell me, what is this work?
en: Then, let us try together. I'd regard it as such an honour. Tell me, what is this work?

text: A Mass. A Mass for the Dead.
en: A Mass. A Mass for the Dead.

text: I want to go!
en: I want to go!

text: Where?
en: Where?

text: I want to go back to Vienna.
en: I want to go back to Vienna.

text: Now?
en: Now?

text: Yes!
en: Yes!

text: Why?
en: Why?

text: I feel wrong. I feel wrong being here.
en: I feel wrong. I feel wrong being here.

text: What are you talking about?
en: What are you talking about?

text: The end of the Recordare - Statuens in parte dextra.
en: The end of the Recordare - Statuens in parte dextra.

text: So now the Confutatis. Confutatis Maledictis. When the wicked are confounded. Flammis acribus addictis. How would you translate that?
en: So now the Confutatis. Confutatis Maledictis. When the wicked are confounded. Flammis acribus addictis. How would you translate that?

text: Consigned to flames of woe.
en: Consigned to flames of woe.

text: Do you believe in it?
en: Do you believe in it?

text: What?
en: What?

text: A fire which never dies. Burning one forever?
en: A fire which never dies. Burning one forever?

text: Oh, yes.
en: Oh, yes.

text: Strange!
en: Strange!

text: Come. Let's begin.
en: Come. Let's begin.

text: We ended in F Major?
en: We ended in F Major?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: So now - A minor. Suddenly.
en: So now - A minor. Suddenly.

text: What time?
en: What time?

text: Common time.
en: Common time.

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Third measure, second beat starting on E.  Flam-mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis.  And fourth measure, fourth beat - D.  Ma-le-dic-tis, flam-mis a-cri-bus ad- dic-tis.  Do you have that?
en: Third measure, second beat starting on E.  Flam-mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis.  And fourth measure, fourth beat - D.  Ma-le-dic-tis, flam-mis a-cri-bus ad- dic-tis.  Do you have that?

text: I think so.
en: I think so.

text: Sing it back.
en: Sing it back.

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Fourth measure, second beat - F.  Flam-mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis, flam- mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis.
en: Fourth measure, second beat - F.  Flam-mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis, flam- mis a-cri-bus ad-dic-tis.

text: Please! Just one moment.
en: Please! Just one moment.

text: First bassoon and tenor trombone - what?
en: First bassoon and tenor trombone - what?

text: With the tenors.
en: With the tenors.

text: Also identical?
en: Also identical?

text: Exactly. The instruments to go with the voices. Trumpets and timpani, tonic and dominant.
en: Exactly. The instruments to go with the voices. Trumpets and timpani, tonic and dominant.

text: Oh no. Now for the Fire.  Strings in unison - ostinato on all - like this.
en: Oh no. Now for the Fire.  Strings in unison - ostinato on all - like this.

text: I think so.
en: I think so.

text: Show me.
en: Show me.

text: Yes, yes - go on. The Voca Me. Suddenly sotto voce. Write that down: sotto voce, pianissimo. Voca me cum benedictis. Call me among the blessed.
en: Yes, yes - go on. The Voca Me. Suddenly sotto voce. Write that down: sotto voce, pianissimo. Voca me cum benedictis. Call me among the blessed.

text: Sopranos up to F on the second 'Voca'?
en: Sopranos up to F on the second 'Voca'?

text: Yes, and on 'dictis'.
en: Yes, and on 'dictis'.

text: Yes!
en: Yes!

text: You go fast!
en: You go fast!

text: Do you have it?
en: Do you have it?

text: Yes.
en: Yes.

text: Then let me hear it. All of it. The whole thing from the beginning - now!
en: Then let me hear it. All of it. The whole thing from the beginning - now!

text: Oh no. I'm not tired at all.
en: Oh no. I'm not tired at all.

text: We'll stop for just a moment. Then we'll do the Lacrimosa.
en: We'll stop for just a moment. Then we'll do the Lacrimosa.

text: I can keep going, I assure you. Shall we try?
en: I can keep going, I assure you. Shall we try?

text: Would you stay with me while I sleep a little?
en: Would you stay with me while I sleep a little?

text: I'm not leaving you.
en: I'm not leaving you.

text: I am so ashamed.
en: I am so ashamed.

text: What for?
en: What for?

text: I was foolish. I thought you did not care for my work - or me. Forgive me. Forgive me!
en: I was foolish. I thought you did not care for my work - or me. Forgive me. Forgive me!

text: Your husband is ill, ma'am. He took sick. I brought him home.
en: Your husband is ill, ma'am. He took sick. I brought him home.

text: Why you?
en: Why you?

text: I was at hand.
en: I was at hand.

text: Well, thank you very much. You can go now.
en: Well, thank you very much. You can go now.

text: He needs me, ma'am.
en: He needs me, ma'am.

text: No, he doesn't. And I don't want you here. Just go, please.
en: No, he doesn't. And I don't want you here. Just go, please.

text: He asked me to stay.
en: He asked me to stay.

text: And I'm asking you -
en: And I'm asking you -

text: No. I was assisting him. He asked me.
en: No. I was assisting him. He asked me.

text: He's not going to work on this anymore. It is making him ill. Please.
en: He's not going to work on this anymore. It is making him ill. Please.

text: Madame, I will respect his. He asked me to stay here.
en: Madame, I will respect his. He asked me to stay here.

text: I did.
en: I did.

text: No, you didn't!
en: No, you didn't!

text: I poisoned his life.
en: I poisoned his life.

text: But not his body.
en: But not his body.

text: What difference does that make?
en: What difference does that make?

text: My son, why should you want all Vienna to believe you a murderer? Is that your penance? Is it?
en: My son, why should you want all Vienna to believe you a murderer? Is that your penance? Is it?

text: No, Father. From now on no one will be able to speak of Mozart without thinking of me. Whenever they say Mozart with love, they'll have to say Salieri with loathing. And that's my immortality - at last! Our names will  be tied together for eternity - his in fame and mine in infamy. At least it's better than the total oblivion he'd planned for me, your merciful God!
en: No, Father. From now on no one will be able to speak of Mozart without thinking of me. Whenever they say Mozart with love, they'll have to say Salieri with loathing. And that's my immortality - at last! Our names will  be tied together for eternity - his in fame and mine in infamy. At least it's better than the total oblivion he'd planned for me, your merciful God!

text: Oh my son, my poor son!
en: Oh my son, my poor son!

text: Don't pity me. Pity yourself. You serve a wicked God. He killed Mozart, not I. Took him, snatched him away, without pity. He destroyed His beloved rather than let a mediocrity like me get the smallest share in his glory. He doesn't care. Understand that. God cares nothing for the man He denies and nothing either for the man He uses. He broke Mozart in half when He'd finished with him, and threw him away. Like an old, worn out flute.
en: Don't pity me. Pity yourself. You serve a wicked God. He killed Mozart, not I. Took him, snatched him away, without pity. He destroyed His beloved rather than let a mediocrity like me get the smallest share in his glory. He doesn't care. Understand that. God cares nothing for the man He denies and nothing either for the man He uses. He broke Mozart in half when He'd finished with him, and threw him away. Like an old, worn out flute.
